comments.text: It's called the Sea of Cortez. That said, it's dry and deserty, but across the peninsula it's subtropical to tropical. As you drive down there, there is a ferry you drive onto and cross to the other side; it takes 24 hours to cross to the Republic of Mexico

replies.author: @truehorizons66
replies.text:
You're right, the Gulf of California is the official geographical name. The Sea of ​​Cortez is also a historical and commonly used name, especially in travel and maritime documentaries.
We appreciate you pointing this out!

comments.text: Baja California feels like one of those places where the desert, the ocean, and human survival all decided to share the same strange address. comments.author: @truehorizons66
comments.text: BAJA CALIFORNIA: How People Live in Mexico’s Most Mysterious Frontier | Travel Documentary

replies.author: @WorldDiscovered_Official
replies.text: Beautiful documentary. Baja California feels like a place where the desert and the ocean meet in the most dramatic way. What makes it special is not only the wild landscape, but also the quiet stories of the people who live there, surviving between harsh nature, tradition, and the sea. This video really captures the feeling of a mysterious frontier that still feels untouched by time.
